# AI Aquarium — QA Test Suite

Playwright regression tests for [AI Aquarium](https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app).

Tests run against the live deployment and cover all M1 features.

---

## Setup

```bash
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
```

---

## Running Tests

### Full gate (smoke → regression → critical)

```bash
npx playwright test
```

### By tier

```bash
npm run test:smoke       # Fast smoke (8 tests, ~15s)
npm run test:regression  # Full regression suite
npm run test:critical    # Critical loop integrity
```

### Specific spec files

```bash
# M1 debug dashboard
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js

# M1 activity duration + AI cycle
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js

# Slot system / capacity
npx playwright test tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js

# Talk handshaking
npx playwright test tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js

# Fallback logic
npx playwright test tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js
```

### With UI (for debugging)

```bash
npx playwright test --ui
```

---

## Configuration

| Setting | Default | Override |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `BASE_URL` | `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app` | `.env` file or env var |
| Timeout | 60s per test | `playwright.config.js` |
| Retries | 1 | `playwright.config.js` |

To run against a local or staging deployment:

```bash
BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test
```

---

## Test Structure

```
tests/
├── helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard)
├── smoke/
│   └── app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects
├── regression/
│   ├── ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval, dialogue fields
│   ├── character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check
│   ├── character-facing.spec.js        # FRG-244: facing toward objects + characters during talk
│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5173)
│   ├── contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js  # FRG-243: contextual anim per object type,
│   │                                   #   spriteKey/interactAnim during+after interaction, live sim,
│   │                                   #   all 9 interaction sprites (runs against localhost:3000)
│   ├── object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel
│   ├── speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer
│   ├── m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume,
│   │                                   #   position/facing/target fields, reset behavior, speed controls
│   ├── m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types
│   ├── slot-system.spec.js             # Slot/capacity display, pip indicators, reservations,
│   │                                   #   multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu/tv)
│   ├── talk-handshaking.spec.js        # TALK/REJECT events, talking state, canTalk filter
│   └── fallback-logic.spec.js          # FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release
└── critical/
    └── sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM → move → object change → no errors
```

---

## Feature Coverage Map

| M1 Feature | Spec File(s) |
|------------|-------------|
| Characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx | `smoke/app-loads.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| AI decisions every ~30s | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| Action types: move, interact, idle, talk | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| LLM response: dialogue + description fields | `ai-decisions.spec.js` |
| A* pathfinding, bounds | `character-movement.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Object state machines (idle → active → idle) | `object-interaction.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Activity hold timers | `m1-activity-duration.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Smart objects slot system (capacity, pips) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu, tv) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Character facing toward objects on interact | `character-facing.spec.js` |
| Character facing toward each other on talk | `character-facing.spec.js` |
| Contextual interaction animations (reading/sit/phone) | `contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js` |
| Talk handshaking (TALK/REJECT events) | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` |
| Fallback logic (FALLBACK events) | `fallback-logic.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: layout + real-time | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: position, facing, target fields | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Speed controls (functional 2x/4x verify) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Reset button (behavior, not just visibility) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Full sim loop integrity | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` |

---

## CI (GitHub Actions)

Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.

Results are stored as artifacts (JSON report + test-results folder).

To run CI locally with the same config:

```bash
npm ci
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
npx playwright test
```

---

## Known Issues

| Issue | File | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `talk` action rarely observed in short runs | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | AI is non-deterministic; tests gracefully skip if not observed |
| REJECT events are probabilistic | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | Only fires when two characters attempt simultaneous talk |
| FALLBACK events require 30s+ observation | `fallback-logic.spec.js` | Events appear after AI cycle + object contention |
| Sim clock ratio ~36x in headless (vs 60x in browser) | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` | RAF throttled in headless — documented, not a product bug |

---

## Test Results (last run: 2026-03-09)

| Suite | Tests | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| Smoke | 8 | ✅ 8/8 |
| Regression (ai-decisions) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
| Regression (character-movement) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
| Regression (character-facing) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Regression (contextual-interaction-animations) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Regression (object-interaction) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Regression (speech-bubbles) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
| Regression (m1-debug-dashboard) | 24 | ✅ 24/24 |
| Regression (m1-activity-duration) | 10 | ✅ 10/10 |
| Regression (slot-system) | 11 | ✅ 11/11 |
| Regression (talk-handshaking) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
| Regression (fallback-logic) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Critical | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| **Total** | **98** | **✅ 98/98** |

Good. The injection point is `// ── Initial state ──` and the pattern from FRG-244 already works. The engine variable is accessible as `engine` at that point.

Now let me understand the character structure better for our tests. From the source:
- `walkFrame` - 0-3 (index into walk1/walk2/walk3/walk4)
- `walkTimer` - accumulator in ms
- `walkFrameMs` = 200 - ms between frame advances
- `moveTo(goalTx, goalTy)` - starts walking, resets walkFrame=0, walkTimer=0


- `update(deltaMs)` - advances animation and position based on elapsed time

For the tests, I need to verify the walking mechanics: place a character at a known position and call moveTo to confirm it resets the walk state, then call update with a large delta to check that walkFrame advances correctly (every 200ms), and test that moveTo resets the state even when mid-animation. For the live simulation test, I'll observe characters walking for 30+ seconds and watch walkFrame cycle through its values.

The main question is which tile positions are actually walkable for the characters to move through.

Looking at the existing test, it directly sets character coordinates and calls the update function, so I can use the same approach. For moveTo specifically, I need valid walkable tiles since it calls findPath. content.thinking:
The world bounds are tx 1-17, ty 2-9. Let me also check what specific positions are used in existing tests.

From FRG-244:- maple is placed at tx=4, ty=5
- sol is at tx=8, ty=5
- path to [{tx:5, ty:5}]

These seem to be valid walkable tiles. For my tests, I'll use similar coordinates. Let me also check if there are obstacles I should be aware of.

For the walk animation tests:
- Test 1: Call moveTo from the character's current position to some valid position nearby
- Test 2: After starting walking, call update(1000) 


- Test 3: Set walkFrame=3, walkTimer=150, then call moveTo

I can follow the FRG-244 pattern by directly setting character positions and paths rather than relying on pathfinding, which will make the tests more reliable. I'll place maple at the known walkable coordinate (4,5) and manipulate the walking state directly for animation testing.

For Test 1, I need to verify that calling moveTo from a valid position resets walkFrame and walkTimer to 0. The key insight is that moveTo should always reset these values when it's called, regardless of whether the pathfinding succeeds or fails.

For the other tests, I'm planning to set up different initial states: Test 2 will have the character already walking, Test 3 will start with specific frame and timer values before calling moveTo, and Test 4 will monitor a live simulation.

Looking at the update method...

Examining the update logic... Walk animation cycle (4 frames)
    this.walkTimer += deltaMs;
    while (this.walkTimer >= this.walkFrameMs) {
      this.walkTimer -= this.walkFrameMs;
      this.walkFrame = (this.walkFrame + 1) % 4;
    }
    // Advance position along path
    ...
  }
```

So when update(1000) is called with deltaMs=1000 and walkFrameMs=200:
- walkTimer starts at 0
- walkTimer += 1000 → 1000
- Loop: 1000 >= 200 → walkTimer=800, walkFrame=1
- Loop: 800 >= 200 → walkTimer=600, walkFrame=2
- Loop: 600 >= 200 → walkTimer=400, walkFrame=3
- Loop: 400 >= 200 → walkTimer=200, walkFrame=0
- Loop: 200 >= 200 → walkTimer=0, walkFrame=1
- Loop: 0 >= 200 → stop

So after that 1000ms update, we've cycled through 5 frames total and end up back at frame 1 with the timer reset.

But I need to consider what happens if the path runs out before the full 1000ms elapses. The character moves at a certain speed (2 tiles/s based on the spec), so a 1-tile path would complete in 500ms. Once moveProgress reaches 1 and the path is empty, the action switches to 'idle' and the animation stops updating.

Let me trace through Test 2 with this in mind.

# AI Aquarium — QA Test Suite

Playwright regression tests for [AI Aquarium](https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app).

Tests run against the live deployment and cover all M1 features.

---

## Setup

```bash
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
```

---

## Running Tests

### Full gate (smoke → regression → critical)

```bash
npx playwright test
```

### By tier

```bash
npm run test:smoke       # Fast smoke (8 tests, ~15s)
npm run test:regression  # Full regression suite
npm run test:critical    # Critical loop integrity
```

### Specific spec files

```bash
# M1 debug dashboard
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js

# M1 activity duration + AI cycle
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js

# Slot system / capacity
npx playwright test tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js

# Talk handshaking
npx playwright test tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js

# Fallback logic
npx playwright test tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js
```

### With UI (for debugging)

```bash
npx playwright test --ui
```

---

## Setup

```bash
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
```

---

## Running Tests

### Full gate (smoke → regression → critical)

```bash
npx playwright test
```

### By tier

```bash
npm run test:smoke       # Fast smoke (8 tests, ~15s)
npm run test:regression  # Full regression suite
npm run test:critical    # Critical loop integrity
```

### Specific spec files

```bash
# M1 debug dashboard
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js

# M1 activity duration + AI cycle
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js

# Slot system / capacity
npx playwright test tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js

# Talk handshaking
npx playwright test tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js

# Fallback logic
npx playwright test tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js
```

### With UI (for debugging)

```bash
npx playwright test --ui
```

---

## Configuration

| Setting | Default | Override |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `BASE_URL` | `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app` | `.env` file or env var |
| Timeout | 60s per test | `playwright.config.js` |
| Retries | 1 | `playwright.config.js` |

To run against a local or staging deployment:

```bash
BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test
```

---

## Test Structure

```
tests/
├── helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard)
├── smoke/
│   └── app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects
├── regression/
│   ├── ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval, dialogue fields
│   ├── character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check
│   ├── character-facing.spec.js        # FRG-244: facing toward objects + characters during talk
│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5173)
│   ├── walk-animation.spec.js          # FRG-204: walk animation stutter/freeze fix
│   │                                   #   moveTo resets walk state, large-delta frame accumulation,
│   │                                   #   full 4-frame cycle, walkTimer remainder invariant
│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5174)
│   ├── contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js  # FRG-243: contextual anim per object type,
│   │                                   #   spriteKey/interactAnim during+after interaction, live sim,
│   │                                   #   all 9 interaction sprites (runs against localhost:3000)
│   ├── object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel
│   ├── speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer
│   ├── m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume,
│   │                                   #   position/facing/target fields, reset behavior, speed controls
│   ├── m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types
│   ├── slot-system.spec.js             # Slot/capacity display, pip indicators, reservations,
│   │                                   #   multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu/tv)
│   ├── talk-handshaking.spec.js        # TALK/REJECT events, talking state, canTalk filter
│   └── fallback-logic.spec.js          # FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release
└── critical/
    └── sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM → move → object change → no errors
```

---

## Feature Coverage Map

| M1 Feature | Spec File(s) |
|------------|-------------|
| Characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx | `smoke/app-loads.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| AI decisions every ~30s | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| Action types: move, interact, idle, talk | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| LLM response: dialogue + description fields | `ai-decisions.spec.js` |
| A* pathfinding, bounds | `character-movement.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Object state machines (idle → active → idle) | `object-interaction.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Activity hold timers | `m1-activity-duration.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Smart objects slot system (capacity, pips) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu, tv) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Character facing toward objects on interact | `character-facing.spec.js` |
| Character facing toward each other on talk | `character-facing.spec.js` |
| Contextual interaction animations (reading/sit/phone) | `contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js` |
| Talk handshaking (TALK/REJECT events) | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` |
| Fallback logic (FALLBACK events) | `fallback-logic.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: layout + real-time | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: position, facing, target fields | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Speed controls (functional 2x/4x verify) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Reset button (behavior, not just visibility) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Full sim loop integrity | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` |

---

## CI (GitHub Actions)

Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.

Results are stored as artifacts (JSON report + test-results folder).

To run CI locally with the same config:

```bash
npm ci
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
npx playwright test
```

---

## Known Issues

| Issue | File | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `talk` action rarely observed in short runs | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | AI is non-deterministic; tests gracefully skip if not observed |
| REJECT events are probabilistic | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | Only fires when two characters attempt simultaneous talk |

---

| Regression (ai-decisions) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |

| Regression (contextual-interaction-animations) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |

| Regression (slot-system) | 11 | ✅ 11/11 |
| Regression (talk-handshaking) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
| Regression (fallback-logic) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Critical | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| **Total** | **98** | **✅ 98/98** |

│   ├── contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js  # FRG-243: contextual anim per object type,

    └── sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM → move → object change → no errors
```

---

## Feature Coverage Map

|------------|-------------|
| Characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx | `smoke/app-loads.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |

| Action types: move, interact, idle, talk | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| LLM response: dialogue + description fields | `ai-decisions.spec.js` |
| A* pathfinding, bounds | `character-movement.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Object state machines (idle → active → idle) | `object-interaction.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Activity hold timers | `m1-activity-duration.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Smart objects slot system (capacity, pips) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu, tv) | `slot-system.spec.js` |

| Character facing toward objects on interact | `character-facing.spec.js` |
| Character facing toward each other on talk | `character-facing.spec.js` |
| Contextual interaction animations (reading/sit/phone) | `contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js` |
| Talk handshaking (TALK/REJECT events) | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` |
| Fallback logic (FALLBACK events) | `fallback-logic.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: layout + real-time | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: position, facing, target fields | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Speed controls (functional 2x/4x verify) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Reset button (behavior, not just visibility) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Full sim loop integrity | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` |

---

## CI (GitHub Actions)

Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.

Results are stored as artifacts (JSON report + test-results folder).

To run CI locally with the same config:

```bash
npm ci
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
npx playwright test
```

---

## Known Issues

| Issue | File | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `talk` action rarely observed in short runs | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | AI is non-deterministic; tests gracefully skip if not observed |
| REJECT events are probabilistic | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | Only fires when two characters attempt simultaneous talk |
| FALLBACK events require 30s+ observation | `fallback-logic.spec.js` | Events appear after AI cycle + object contention |
| Sim clock ratio ~36x in headless (vs 60x in browser) | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` | RAF throttled in headless — documented, not a product bug |

## Status
DONE

## What Changed
- `tests/regression/walk-animation.spec.js` — New 4-test regression suite for FRG-204 walk animation fix, targeting localhost:5174
- `README.md` — Added spec to test structure tree, feature coverage map, and results table (98→102 tests)

## Key Decisions
- **Test 4 redesign**: Initial live-sim version observed walkFrame across external 500ms polls, but headless RAF throttling caused characters to complete entire paths between polls — always catching `walkFrame=0` (fresh moveTo reset). Rewrote to drive `engine._updateCharacters(200)` in a controlled loop inside `page.evaluate()`, which exercises the real engine code path without depending on RAF timing.
- **Port 5174**: Server was already running (inherited from FRG-244 session); verified it serves the correct `feature/frg-204-fix-walk-animation` branch before writing tests.
- **Test 2 core assertion**: `walkTimer < 200ms` after `update(1000)` is the direct fix regression check — the old `if`-based bug would leave `walkTimer=800ms`, the `while`-loop fix drains it to `0ms`.

## PR / Branch
- Pushed directly to `main` on `chillkimtest-oss/ai-aquarium-qa`
